spec_match.rationale: Direction is REVERSED. Page says `old: NotAllowedError → new: SecurityError`. Per the feature title and chromestatus, the actual change is SecurityError → NotAllowedError. The linter therefore flags correct (post-135) code as wrong and tells authors to keep the broken pre-135 catch.

interactive.rationale: A `<textarea>` paste-and-scan linter that fires on input, parses for `credentials.create...payment` and the two error.name string literals, and renders findings live.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Lede promises a side-by-side before/after with a live linter — delivered, but with the rename direction backwards. Lede also says `the rest of WebAuthn throws SecurityError in the same situation`, which is incorrect: non-payment WebAuthn throws NotAllowedError without activation.

edge_cases.rationale: Linter looks for the two literal strings; misses cases where the name is held in a variable, where catches use `instanceof DOMException`, or where the catch lives in a function called from the try.

design_system.rationale: Variables only, matrix grid responsive, findings panel uses ok/bad semantic states, no static cards.

openQuestions.title: Reverse the linter's expectation
openQuestions.detail: The page tells authors that pre-135 catches checked NotAllowedError and 135+ should check SecurityError. The actual fix is the other way: pre-135 payment threw SecurityError, 135+ throws NotAllowedError (matching the rest of WebAuthn). Reverse the matrix headings, the `old:` / `new:` pills, and the scan logic so it flags `branchesOnSecurity && !branchesOnNotAllowed` as the warning case.

openQuestions.title: Fix the lede's claim about non-payment WebAuthn
openQuestions.detail: Lede says `the rest of WebAuthn throws SecurityError in the same situation`. That's wrong — non-payment WebAuthn create() without a gesture throws NotAllowedError. That's why the spec alignment landed: payment was the odd-one-out throwing SecurityError. Fix the lede to say `the rest of WebAuthn throws NotAllowedError in the same situation`.

openQuestions.title: Add AST-based scanning, not string match
openQuestions.detail: The linter does a regex over the source. Add a fallback that parses with the browser's `Function()` constructor wrapped in try (to syntax-check) or pull in a small JS parser (acorn ~25KB) so it can detect catches that bind the error to a variable and switch on it.

openQuestions.title: Link to the W3C spec PR that landed the alignment
openQuestions.detail: The fix landed in https://github.com/w3c/secure-payment-confirmation/pull/281 (referenced from the feature index). Link to it from this page too so authors can see the canonical change.

summary: Genuinely useful tooling shape — paste your catch, get told if it's wrong — but the page has the rename DIRECTION reversed, so the linter actively tells authors to keep the broken pre-135 catch. Plus the lede's claim about the rest of WebAuthn is wrong. Two major factual errors that need fixing before this is shippable.
